column2: What is the primary purpose of an operating system?
column4: An operating system coordinates hardware resources, applications, and user interaction so the computer can run usable workloads.
column5: The exam is not asking for one narrow feature such as a desktop or file manager. The OS is the control layer between hardware, software, and the user.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 1
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic HighYield OS Source::Messer-v140

column2: A business needs broad desktop software support and common industry hardware compatibility. Which workstation OS family is the most likely fit?
column5: Windows has a large software and hardware ecosystem. The tradeoff is that its large install base and wide hardware coverage can also increase security and integration complexity.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 1
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic Windows OS Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which workstation OS family is Unix-like, has many distributions, and is often chosen for low cost and hardware flexibility?
column5: Linux is not one single desktop product. Support, drivers, and administration experience can vary by distribution and hardware platform.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 1
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic Linux OS Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which workstation OS is tied to Apple hardware and is commonly selected for an Apple-managed desktop environment?
column5: The key distinction is platform coupling. macOS can be a strong fit inside an Apple environment, but it is not a general-purpose OS for arbitrary PC hardware.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 1
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic macOS OS Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which workstation OS is centered on the Chrome browser and is best matched to a cloud-heavy, web-application workflow?
column5: Chrome OS is based on the Linux kernel, but the exam distinction is its browser-centered and cloud-dependent workflow.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 1
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic ChromeOS Cloud Source::Messer-v140

column2: How do iOS, iPadOS, and Android differ by supported device platform?
column4: iOS runs on Apple iPhones, iPadOS runs on Apple iPad tablets, and Android runs on many manufacturers' mobile devices.
column5: The point is platform recognition, not app development. iOS and iPadOS are Apple-specific, while Android is the non-Apple mobile OS family with broad manufacturer support.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 1-2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic MobileOS Apple Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why must a partition be formatted with a filesystem before it stores normal files?
column4: Formatting creates the filesystem structure the operating system uses to organize files and metadata on that partition.
column5: The filesystem is not just a label. It defines how data is arranged and which OS features and compatibility limits apply to the storage device.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2

column2: Why is NTFS a poor first choice for a removable drive that must be written by Windows, macOS, and Linux systems?
column4: NTFS is a Windows-focused filesystem, and non-Windows systems may have limited or read-only NTFS support.
column5: NTFS is common and capable on Windows, but cross-platform write compatibility is the trap. A scenario about easy file movement across operating systems points away from NTFS.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic HighYield Filesystems Windows Compatibility Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Windows filesystem is most associated with large storage requirements and ongoing data integrity checks?
column5: ReFS is not the general cross-platform answer. It is the Windows-side choice when the requirement points to large storage and resilience rather than broad OS compatibility.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic HighYield Filesystems Windows Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user needs a USB flash drive for files larger than 4 GB and wants to move the drive between Windows, Linux, and macOS. Which filesystem is the best fit?
column5: FAT32 is broadly recognized but has a 4 GB maximum file size. exFAT is the better removable-media fit when both large files and cross-OS compatibility matter.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic HighYield Filesystems Compatibility Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which filesystem is commonly associated with Linux and Android systems?
column5: ext4 stands for fourth extended filesystem. It is the Linux/Android association to recognize, not the Apple or Windows storage answer.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic Filesystems Linux Android Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Linux filesystem is designed for high performance and scalability on large filesystems?
column5: XFS is a Linux-oriented answer for large, high-performance storage. Do not mix it up with ReFS, which is the Windows large-storage resilience option in this objective.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic Filesystems Linux Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which filesystem is the Apple platform choice for modern macOS, iOS, and iPadOS storage?
column5: APFS is the Apple filesystem association. It is optimized for modern Apple storage features, including solid-state storage and data integrity capabilities.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic HighYield Filesystems Apple macOS Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why is an end-of-life operating system a support risk?
column4: The vendor may stop providing normal updates, leaving security and compatibility problems that cannot be fixed through routine patching.
column5: End-of-life is a vendor lifecycle state, not a local setting. A technician may need to recommend an upgrade or replacement instead of expecting future patches.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic HighYield Lifecycle Security Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why does moving a native application from one operating system to another often fail even when the user files can be copied?
column4: Native applications are usually built for a specific OS platform, while many data files or web-based services are more portable across systems.
column5: The exam distinction is application compatibility versus data portability. A file may move successfully even when the application that created it cannot run on the destination OS.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column10: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Basic HighYield Compatibility OS Source::Messer-v140
